recommendations: deduplicate and namespace-colliding MCP endpoints (e.g.
repeated `get_structure`
`set_structure`
`search`
`query`) to avoid ambiguous tool resolution
add contract tests for every generated MCP endpoint with smoke tests for required/optional dependency permutations (numpy-only vs scipy/matplotlib/requests/etc.)
introduce strict input/output schemas with Pydantic models (including ndarray/structure serialization rules) and consistent error envelopes
split very large high-churn modules behind thinner MCP-facing façade functions to reduce coupling and breakage risk
add CI matrix jobs that validate the plugin in `import` and fallback modes across supported Python versions
implement endpoint-level timeouts/retries/rate-limiting for remote database calls (RCSB/UniProt/Entrez/PubChem) plus cache controls
add golden-file regression tests for core scientific outputs (alignment scores
RMSD/superposition transforms
parsed structure counts)
enforce API naming conventions (verb_noun + module prefix) and auto-generate an endpoint catalog from docstrings
improve observability with structured logs and per-endpoint latency/error metrics
harden security by validating file paths and sanitizing external query parameters before execution
expand README_MCP with dependency tiers
failure modes
and reproducible local run examples
add benchmark-driven performance gates for heavy endpoints (alignment
PDBx conversion
PyMOL object ops)
include compatibility shims/tests for optional integrations (rdkit/openmm/pymol) to fail gracefully when unavailable
pin and periodically audit MCP plugin dependencies (`fastmcp`
`pydantic`) with automated update PRs
add property-based tests for parsers/converters (FASTA/PDB/PDBx/GenBank/GFF) to catch edge-case serialization bugs

success_factors: Workflow completed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize) with final status = success.
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for this repo (import feasibility 0.9, confidence 0.93, low intrusiveness risk).
Repository structure and metadata were sufficient for automated endpoint extraction from core biotite modules.
Generated MCP service passed plugin health checks (healthy over stdio), indicating runnable baseline service.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing external architectural/context enrichment.
Original project test result is marked as not passed with empty details, suggesting tests were not actually executed/validated in this run.
Several metrics are zero/unknown (LOC, file size, performance counters, versions), indicating observability gaps rather than runtime failure.

node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded with 7 files created and endpoint surface produced; per-node timing unavailable.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed; original project test status is inconclusive/not passed with no diagnostics. Timing fields reported as 0.
